issues.description: Misleading/redundant `result=$?` pattern in M-size and L-size quality loop tests (lines 279-290). The `should_run_quality_loop "M"` call without `run` means BATS will abort the test at that line if it returns non-zero — so `result` is always 0, and the subsequent `[ "$result" -eq 0 ]` assertion is tautological and never catches a real failure. This creates false confidence. Contrast with the correct S-size test at line 274 which properly uses `run should_run_quality_loop "S"` and then checks `$status`. The M and L tests should use the same pattern: `run should_run_quality_loop "M"` then `[ "$status" -eq 0 ]`.

Same `result=$?` anti-pattern in `should_run_docs_stage runs for typescript changes` (lines 749-754). The bash and config variants correctly use `run should_run_docs_stage` + `$status`, but the typescript variant uses the verbose `$?` capture pattern which is always 0. This inconsistency within the same section is confusing. Should be: `run should_run_docs_stage "typescript"` then `[ "$status" -eq 0 ]`.

Test name `detect_change_scope classifies typescript files` is actively misleading — the body only checks `type -t detect_change_scope` (function existence), it performs zero classification testing. The detailed classification tests live in `test-smart-test-targeting.bats`, so coverage exists, but this test name creates a false impression of what is being verified. Either rename the test to `detect_change_scope function exists` or remove it entirely since existence is already implicit from other tests in `test-smart-test-targeting.bats` that call the function.

Overly broad exit code assertions in the ERROR HANDLING section. `orchestrator exits 1 on parse_issue failure` (line 507) and `orchestrator exits 2 on max PR review iterations` (line 529) both check `[[ "$main_def" == *"exit 1"* ]]` / `[[ "$main_def" == *"exit 2"* ]]` against the entire `main` function body. Since `main` has multiple `exit 1` and `exit 2` calls for different failure modes, these tests pass regardless of whether the specific scenario's exit path is present. They'd still pass if the PR review max-iterations exit was accidentally removed. Consider narrowing by checking the surrounding context string rather than just the exit code in isolation, or delegate these assertions to the behavioral tests below.

Duplicate schema copying in `setup()`. The helper's `setup_test_env()` (test-helper.bash lines 30-33) already copies schemas from `$SCRIPT_DIR/schemas` into `$TEST_TMP/schemas`. Then `setup()` in this file does the same copy again at lines 28-29. The second copy is harmless but unnecessary — it adds noise and could cause confusion about where schema setup responsibility lives. Remove the duplicate block (lines 28-39 can be simplified to just the fallback minimal schema creation, since `setup_test_env` handles the real copy).
